passage_text:
The shift to remote work, which accelerated dramatically during the global pandemic, has fundamentally transformed how millions of professionals conduct their daily lives. A survey conducted by the Global Workforce Institute in 2023 revealed that approximately 42 percent of full-time employees in developed countries now work from home at least part of the week. This dramatic shift has prompted urban planners and economists to reassess their assumptions about city design, commuting patterns, and real estate demand.

One of the most significant consequences of widespread remote work has been the so-called 'urban exodus.' As employees gained the freedom to work from anywhere, many opted to leave expensive, densely populated metropolitan areas in favor of smaller cities or suburban communities. Real estate data from several major cities showed vacancy rates in downtown office buildings climbing to record highs, while housing demand surged in previously overlooked rural and semi-urban regions. Critics, however, argue that this trend has exacerbated economic inequality, as lower-income workers who cannot perform their jobs remotely are left behind in struggling city centers.

Despite these challenges, some urban economists remain cautiously optimistic about the long-term impact of remote work on cities. They argue that reduced commuter traffic has led to measurable improvements in air quality and a decline in carbon emissions within city limits. Furthermore, the redistribution of population from crowded urban centers to smaller communities is seen by some as a positive step toward more balanced regional development. Local governments in smaller towns have also reported increased tax revenues as new residents bring economic activity to previously underdeveloped areas.

However, the future of remote work remains uncertain. Several major corporations have recently reversed their work-from-home policies, requiring employees to return to the office at least three days per week. Proponents of this shift argue that in-person collaboration fosters creativity and strengthens workplace culture, while remote work advocates counter that forced returns undermine employee satisfaction and productivity. The ongoing debate reflects a broader tension in modern workplaces between organizational efficiency and individual autonomy.

questions.quiz: What did the 2023 Global Workforce Institute survey reveal?
questions.quiz_ko: 2023년 글로벌 인력 연구소 조사에서 밝혀진 것은?
questions.answer1: Most employees prefer to work in offices full-time.
questions.answer1_ko: 대부분의 직원들은 사무실에서 전일 근무하기를 선호한다.
questions.answer2: About 42 percent of full-time employees work remotely at least part of the week.
questions.answer2_ko: 전일제 직원의 약 42%가 주 중 최소 일부를 재택근무한다.
questions.answer3: Remote work has caused productivity to decline significantly.
questions.answer3_ko: 재택근무로 인해 생산성이 크게 감소했다.
questions.answer4: Urban planners support a complete shift to remote work.
questions.answer4_ko: 도시 계획가들은 완전한 재택근무 전환을 지지한다.
questions.commentary: 1단락에 'approximately 42 percent of full-time employees in developed countries now work from home at least part of the week'라고 직접 언급되어 있습니다. approximately(약, 대략)는 수치를 나타낼 때 자주 쓰이는 부사로 꼭 기억해 두세요.

questions.quiz: What does the 'urban exodus' described in paragraph 2 refer to?
questions.quiz_ko: 2단락에서 언급된 '도시 탈출'이 가리키는 것은?
questions.answer1: An increase in the number of people moving into city centers.
questions.answer1_ko: 도심으로 이주하는 사람들의 증가.
questions.answer2: A sharp rise in office building construction in metropolitan areas.
questions.answer2_ko: 대도시 지역의 사무용 건물 건설 급증.
questions.answer3: A government policy requiring workers to leave major cities.
questions.answer3_ko: 노동자들이 주요 도시를 떠나도록 요구하는 정부 정책.
questions.answer4: Employees leaving expensive cities to live in smaller communities.
questions.answer4_ko: 직원들이 비싼 도시를 떠나 소규모 지역 사회로 이주하는 현상.
questions.commentary: 2단락에 'many opted to leave expensive, densely populated metropolitan areas in favor of smaller cities or suburban communities'라고 나옵니다. opted to(~을 선택했다)는 chose to와 동의어입니다. in favor of(~을 선호하여)도 지텔프에서 자주 나오는 표현입니다.

questions.quiz: According to paragraph 3, what environmental benefit has remote work produced?
questions.quiz_ko: 3단락에 따르면, 재택근무가 가져온 환경적 이점은?
questions.answer1: Improved air quality and reduced carbon emissions in cities.
questions.answer1_ko: 도시 내 대기질 개선과 탄소 배출 감소.
questions.answer2: A significant decrease in the amount of household waste produced.
questions.answer2_ko: 가정에서 배출되는 쓰레기 양의 상당한 감소.
questions.answer3: An increase in green spaces within urban centers.
questions.answer3_ko: 도심 내 녹지 공간 증가.
questions.answer4: A reduction in the use of single-use plastics in offices.
questions.answer4_ko: 사무실에서의 일회용 플라스틱 사용 감소.
questions.commentary: 3단락에 'reduced commuter traffic has led to measurable improvements in air quality and a decline in carbon emissions'라고 나옵니다. lead to(~으로 이어지다)는 결과를 나타내는 핵심 표현입니다. measurable(측정 가능한, 뚜렷한)도 함께 익혀 두세요.

questions.quiz: According to paragraph 3, how have smaller towns benefited from the population shift?
questions.quiz_ko: 3단락에 따르면, 소규모 도시들은 인구 이동으로 어떤 혜택을 받았나?
questions.answer1: They received funding from national governments to build new infrastructure.
questions.answer1_ko: 새로운 인프라 건설을 위해 중앙 정부로부터 자금을 받았다.
questions.answer2: They were able to attract large corporations to open new offices.
questions.answer2_ko: 대기업들이 새로운 사무소를 개설하도록 유치할 수 있었다.
questions.answer3: They reported increased tax revenues as new residents brought economic activity.
questions.answer3_ko: 새 주민들이 경제 활동을 가져오면서 세수 증가를 보고했다.
questions.answer4: They developed new public transportation networks to handle population growth.
questions.answer4_ko: 인구 증가를 처리하기 위해 새로운 대중교통망을 개발했다.
questions.commentary: 3단락에 'Local governments in smaller towns have also reported increased tax revenues as new residents bring economic activity to previously underdeveloped areas'라고 나옵니다. as는 '~때문에, ~하면서'의 의미로 여기서는 이유를 나타냅니다.

questions.quiz: What argument do proponents of returning to the office make?
questions.quiz_ko: 사무실 복귀 지지자들이 주장하는 것은?
questions.answer1: Remote work allows employees to maintain better work-life balance.
questions.answer1_ko: 재택근무는 직원들이 더 나은 일-생활 균형을 유지하게 한다.
questions.answer2: In-person collaboration fosters creativity and strengthens workplace culture.
questions.answer2_ko: 대면 협업이 창의성을 높이고 직장 문화를 강화한다.
questions.answer3: Office buildings generate more economic activity for city centers.
questions.answer3_ko: 사무용 건물은 도심에 더 많은 경제 활동을 창출한다.
questions.answer4: Employees are more likely to receive promotions when working in the office.
questions.answer4_ko: 직원들은 사무실에서 근무할 때 승진 가능성이 더 높다.
questions.commentary: 4단락에 'Proponents of this shift argue that in-person collaboration fosters creativity and strengthens workplace culture'라고 나옵니다. proponents(지지자들)는 advocates / supporters와 동의어입니다. foster(촉진하다, 육성하다)는 지텔프에 자주 등장하는 동사입니다.

questions.quiz: The word 'exacerbated' in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to:
questions.quiz_ko: 2단락의 'exacerbated'와 가장 가까운 의미의 단어는?
questions.answer1: relieved
questions.answer1_ko: 완화된
questions.answer2: identified
questions.answer2_ko: 확인된
questions.answer3: worsened
questions.answer3_ko: 악화된
questions.answer4: predicted
questions.answer4_ko: 예측된
questions.commentary: exacerbate는 '악화시키다, 더 심하게 만들다'라는 뜻으로, worsened(악화된)가 동의어입니다. 'this trend has exacerbated economic inequality'는 '이 추세가 경제적 불평등을 악화시켰다'는 의미입니다. 반의어는 alleviate / relieve(완화하다)입니다.

questions.quiz: The word 'autonomy' in paragraph 4 is closest in meaning to:
questions.quiz_ko: 4단락의 'autonomy'와 가장 가까운 의미의 단어는?
questions.answer1: independence
questions.answer1_ko: 독립성, 자율성
questions.answer2: efficiency
questions.answer2_ko: 효율성
questions.answer3: creativity
questions.answer3_ko: 창의성
questions.answer4: obligation
questions.answer4_ko: 의무
questions.commentary: autonomy는 '자율성, 자치권'이라는 뜻으로, independence(독립성)가 동의어입니다. 'tension between organizational efficiency and individual autonomy'는 '조직 효율과 개인 자율 사이의 긴장'이라는 의미입니다. autonomous(자율적인)라는 형용사 형태도 함께 익혀 두세요.

passage_text:
Over the past decade, researchers and mental health professionals have become increasingly concerned about the relationship between social media use and the psychological wellbeing of teenagers. A landmark study published in the Journal of Adolescent Health found that teenagers who spend more than three hours per day on social media platforms are significantly more likely to report symptoms of depression and anxiety than those who limit their usage. The study, which surveyed over 12,000 adolescents aged 13 to 18, concluded that the compulsive nature of social media engagement may be fundamentally altering how young people perceive themselves and others.

One key mechanism driving this association is the phenomenon of 'social comparison.' When teenagers are constantly exposed to carefully curated images of peers appearing to lead more exciting or fulfilling lives, feelings of inadequacy and low self-esteem often follow. Researchers note that unlike in-person social interactions, social media provides a one-sided view of reality that makes unfavorable comparisons almost inevitable. Girls, in particular, are reported to be more vulnerable to these effects, with studies showing higher rates of body image dissatisfaction among regular social media users.

In response, several countries have begun implementing digital literacy programs in schools aimed at helping students critically evaluate the content they consume online. Advocates of these programs argue that equipping young people with the skills to distinguish between curated online personas and real life is the most sustainable long-term solution. Some school districts have even introduced 'phone-free' policies during school hours, which early data suggests may improve student concentration and reduce anxiety levels.

However, critics caution that overly restricting teenagers' access to social media may cut them off from legitimate communication networks and supportive online communities that many rely on for social connection, particularly those in rural or isolated areas. The debate ultimately reflects a broader challenge: how to harness the benefits of digital technology while protecting vulnerable populations from its potential harms.

questions.quiz: What did the study published in the Journal of Adolescent Health find?
questions.quiz_ko: 청소년 건강 저널에 발표된 연구에서 밝혀진 것은?
questions.answer1: Social media use has no measurable effect on teenage mental health.
questions.answer1_ko: 소셜 미디어 사용은 청소년 정신 건강에 측정 가능한 영향을 미치지 않는다.
questions.answer2: Teenagers who use social media are more likely to excel academically.
questions.answer2_ko: 소셜 미디어를 사용하는 청소년들은 학업적으로 뛰어날 가능성이 더 높다.
questions.answer3: Teens spending over 3 hours daily on social media show higher rates of depression and anxiety.
questions.answer3_ko: 하루 3시간 이상 소셜 미디어를 사용하는 청소년은 우울증과 불안 비율이 더 높다.
questions.answer4: Most teenagers are able to use social media without any negative psychological effects.
questions.answer4_ko: 대부분의 청소년은 부정적인 심리적 영향 없이 소셜 미디어를 사용할 수 있다.
questions.commentary: 1단락에 'teenagers who spend more than three hours per day on social media platforms are significantly more likely to report symptoms of depression and anxiety'라고 나옵니다. significantly(상당히, 현저하게)는 정도를 강조하는 부사로 지텔프에 자주 등장합니다.

questions.quiz: According to paragraph 2, what is 'social comparison' and what does it lead to?
questions.quiz_ko: 2단락에 따르면, '사회적 비교'란 무엇이며 어떤 결과를 초래하나?
questions.answer1: Comparing oneself to idealized online images, leading to feelings of inadequacy.
questions.answer1_ko: 이상화된 온라인 이미지와 자신을 비교하여 부족감으로 이어지는 현상.
questions.answer2: Comparing academic performance with classmates, leading to increased motivation.
questions.answer2_ko: 학업 성취도를 반 친구들과 비교하여 동기 부여가 증가하는 현상.
questions.answer3: Evaluating social platforms to choose the most suitable one for communication.
questions.answer3_ko: 소통에 가장 적합한 플랫폼을 선택하기 위해 소셜 플랫폼을 평가하는 것.
questions.answer4: Tracking the number of followers to measure social popularity.
questions.answer4_ko: 사회적 인기를 측정하기 위해 팔로워 수를 추적하는 것.
questions.commentary: 2단락에 'constantly exposed to carefully curated images of peers appearing to lead more exciting or fulfilling lives, feelings of inadequacy and low self-esteem often follow'라고 나옵니다. curated(선별된, 정제된)는 소셜 미디어 맥락에서 자주 쓰이는 어휘입니다. inevitably(불가피하게)도 함께 익혀 두세요.

questions.quiz: According to paragraph 2, which group is reported to be most vulnerable to social media's negative effects?
questions.quiz_ko: 2단락에 따르면, 소셜 미디어의 부정적 영향에 가장 취약한 집단은?
questions.answer1: Boys aged 13 to 15
questions.answer1_ko: 13~15세 남학생
questions.answer2: College-age students who use multiple platforms
questions.answer2_ko: 여러 플랫폼을 사용하는 대학생 연령층
questions.answer3: Children under the age of 10
questions.answer3_ko: 10세 미만의 어린이
questions.answer4: Girls, who show higher rates of body image dissatisfaction.
questions.answer4_ko: 신체 이미지 불만족 비율이 더 높은 여학생.
questions.commentary: 2단락에 'Girls, in particular, are reported to be more vulnerable to these effects, with studies showing higher rates of body image dissatisfaction among regular social media users'라고 나옵니다. in particular(특히)는 특정 집단이나 사례를 강조할 때 쓰입니다. vulnerable(취약한)은 지텔프에 자주 나오는 형용사입니다.

questions.quiz: What solution is proposed in paragraph 3 to address the negative effects of social media?
questions.quiz_ko: 3단락에서 소셜 미디어의 부정적 영향을 해결하기 위해 제안된 방법은?
questions.answer1: Banning teenagers from using social media entirely.
questions.answer1_ko: 청소년의 소셜 미디어 사용을 완전히 금지하는 것.
questions.answer2: Implementing digital literacy programs to help students critically evaluate online content.
questions.answer2_ko: 학생들이 온라인 콘텐츠를 비판적으로 평가하도록 디지털 리터러시 프로그램 도입.
questions.answer3: Requiring social media companies to reduce the amount of advertising.
questions.answer3_ko: 소셜 미디어 회사들이 광고량을 줄이도록 요구하는 것.
questions.answer4: Introducing mandatory counseling sessions for teenagers who use social media.
questions.answer4_ko: 소셜 미디어를 사용하는 청소년을 위한 의무적인 상담 세션 도입.
questions.commentary: 3단락에 'several countries have begun implementing digital literacy programs in schools aimed at helping students critically evaluate the content they consume online'라고 나옵니다. implement(시행하다, 도입하다)는 지텔프에서 solution / policy와 관련된 문제에서 자주 등장하는 동사입니다.

questions.quiz: What concern do critics raise about restricting teenagers' social media access?
questions.quiz_ko: 비평가들이 청소년의 소셜 미디어 접근을 제한하는 것에 대해 제기하는 우려는?
questions.answer1: Restrictions may increase the cost of developing better social platforms.
questions.answer1_ko: 제한이 더 나은 소셜 플랫폼 개발 비용을 증가시킬 수 있다.
questions.answer2: Teenagers may turn to more harmful offline activities without social media.
questions.answer2_ko: 청소년들이 소셜 미디어 없이 더 해로운 오프라인 활동으로 전환할 수 있다.
questions.answer3: Restricting access may cut teens off from legitimate communication and supportive communities.
questions.answer3_ko: 접근 제한이 청소년들을 정당한 소통과 지지 커뮤니티로부터 단절시킬 수 있다.
questions.answer4: Government regulation of social media could violate freedom of speech laws.
questions.answer4_ko: 소셜 미디어에 대한 정부 규제가 언론의 자유 법률을 침해할 수 있다.
questions.commentary: 4단락에 'overly restricting teenagers' access to social media may cut them off from legitimate communication networks and supportive online communities'라고 나옵니다. cut off from(~로부터 단절시키다)은 isolate from과 같은 의미로, 지텔프 독해에서 자주 쓰이는 표현입니다.

questions.quiz: The word 'compulsive' in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to:
questions.quiz_ko: 1단락의 'compulsive'와 가장 가까운 의미의 단어는?
questions.answer1: uncontrollable
questions.answer1_ko: 통제할 수 없는
questions.answer2: creative
questions.answer2_ko: 창의적인
questions.answer3: occasional
questions.answer3_ko: 가끔의
questions.answer4: deliberate
questions.answer4_ko: 의도적인, 신중한
questions.commentary: compulsive는 '강박적인, 충동을 억제하지 못하는'이라는 뜻으로, uncontrollable(통제할 수 없는)이 가장 가까운 의미입니다. 'the compulsive nature of social media engagement'는 '소셜 미디어 참여의 강박적인 특성'이라는 의미입니다. compulsion(강박, 충동)이라는 명사 형태도 기억해 두세요.

questions.quiz: The word 'inadequacy' in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to:
questions.quiz_ko: 2단락의 'inadequacy'와 가장 가까운 의미의 표현은?
questions.answer1: confidence
questions.answer1_ko: 자신감
questions.answer2: curiosity
questions.answer2_ko: 호기심
questions.answer3: creativity
questions.answer3_ko: 창의성
questions.answer4: a sense of not being good enough
questions.answer4_ko: 충분하지 않다는 느낌, 열등감
questions.commentary: inadequacy는 '부족함, 열등감'이라는 뜻으로, 'a sense of not being good enough'가 가장 잘 설명합니다. inadequate(부족한, 불충분한)라는 형용사 형태도 함께 익혀 두세요. 1번 confidence(자신감)는 반의어에 가깝습니다.

passage_text:
As global urbanization continues at an unprecedented pace, cities around the world are facing mounting pressure to secure reliable food supplies for their growing populations. Urban farming — the practice of cultivating, processing, and distributing food in or around urban areas — has emerged as a promising solution to this challenge. From rooftop gardens and community allotments to sophisticated vertical farms, the movement to bring food production closer to consumers is gaining significant momentum in cities across North America, Europe, and Asia.

Vertical farming, in particular, has attracted considerable attention from investors and urban planners. By stacking crops in climate-controlled indoor environments and using LED lighting and hydroponic systems, vertical farms can produce yields up to ten times greater per square meter than conventional outdoor fields. Moreover, these facilities use up to 95 percent less water than traditional agriculture, making them an attractive option in regions facing water scarcity. However, critics point out that the high initial setup costs and significant energy consumption required to power artificial lighting represent major barriers to widespread adoption.

Community gardens and urban allotments, while less technologically intensive, offer their own set of advantages. Studies have shown that participation in community gardening programs improves residents' access to fresh fruits and vegetables, particularly in low-income neighborhoods that are often classified as 'food deserts.' Beyond nutritional benefits, these shared green spaces have been shown to strengthen social bonds among residents and reduce feelings of social isolation. Local governments in cities such as Detroit, Havana, and Singapore have actively promoted community gardening as both a food security strategy and a tool for community development.

Despite its many potential benefits, urban farming alone cannot replace conventional agriculture as the primary source of the world's food supply. Land constraints, high production costs, and energy demands remain significant challenges that must be addressed if urban food systems are to scale up meaningfully. Nevertheless, most experts agree that urban farming represents a valuable supplement to existing food systems, particularly as cities seek more resilient and localized approaches to food security in the face of climate change and supply chain disruptions.

questions.quiz: What is identified as the primary driver behind the growth of urban farming?
questions.quiz_ko: 도시 농업 성장의 주요 동력으로 확인된 것은?
questions.answer1: The need to secure food supplies for growing urban populations.
questions.answer1_ko: 증가하는 도시 인구를 위한 안정적인 식량 공급 확보의 필요성.
questions.answer2: Government subsidies encouraging farmers to move into city centers.
questions.answer2_ko: 농부들이 도심으로 이주하도록 장려하는 정부 보조금.
questions.answer3: Rising consumer demand for organic and locally produced food.
questions.answer3_ko: 유기농 및 지역 생산 식품에 대한 소비자 수요 증가.
questions.answer4: A global decline in the productivity of traditional agricultural land.
questions.answer4_ko: 전통적인 농경지 생산성의 전 세계적 감소.
questions.commentary: 1단락에 'cities around the world are facing mounting pressure to secure reliable food supplies for their growing populations'라고 나옵니다. mounting pressure(증가하는 압박)는 growing pressure와 같은 표현입니다. urbanization(도시화)은 환경·사회 관련 지문에서 자주 등장합니다.

questions.quiz: What water-related advantage of vertical farming is mentioned in paragraph 2?
questions.quiz_ko: 2단락에서 언급된 수직 농업의 물 관련 이점은?
questions.answer1: Vertical farms can collect rainwater to irrigate crops at no additional cost.
questions.answer1_ko: 수직 농장은 추가 비용 없이 빗물을 모아 작물에 사용할 수 있다.
questions.answer2: Vertical farms produce crops that require 50 percent less water to grow.
questions.answer2_ko: 수직 농장은 재배에 50% 적은 물이 필요한 작물을 생산한다.
questions.answer3: Vertical farms can purify and recycle wastewater for surrounding communities.
questions.answer3_ko: 수직 농장은 주변 지역사회를 위해 폐수를 정화하고 재활용할 수 있다.
questions.answer4: Vertical farms use up to 95 percent less water than traditional agriculture.
questions.answer4_ko: 수직 농장은 전통 농업보다 물을 최대 95% 적게 사용한다.
questions.commentary: 2단락에 'these facilities use up to 95 percent less water than traditional agriculture'라고 명시되어 있습니다. up to(최대 ~까지)는 수치를 나타낼 때 자주 쓰이는 표현입니다. hydroponic(수경재배의)은 수직 농업 관련 지문에서 핵심 어휘입니다.

questions.quiz: What limitation of vertical farming is mentioned in paragraph 2?
questions.quiz_ko: 2단락에서 언급된 수직 농업의 한계는?
questions.answer1: Vertical farms can only grow a limited variety of crops.
questions.answer1_ko: 수직 농장은 제한된 종류의 작물만 재배할 수 있다.
questions.answer2: High setup costs and significant energy consumption are major barriers.
questions.answer2_ko: 높은 초기 설치 비용과 상당한 에너지 소비가 주요 장벽이다.
questions.answer3: Vertical farms require large amounts of land that cities cannot provide.
questions.answer3_ko: 수직 농장은 도시가 제공할 수 없는 넓은 토지를 필요로 한다.
questions.answer4: Crops grown in vertical farms are often lower in nutritional value.
questions.answer4_ko: 수직 농장에서 재배된 작물은 종종 영양가가 낮다.
questions.commentary: 2단락에 'the high initial setup costs and significant energy consumption required to power artificial lighting represent major barriers to widespread adoption'라고 나옵니다. represent(나타내다, ~이다)는 지텔프에서 자주 쓰이는 동사입니다. barrier(장벽)는 obstacle / challenge와 동의어입니다.

questions.quiz: According to paragraph 3, what is a 'food desert'?
questions.quiz_ko: 3단락에 따르면, '식량 사막'이란 무엇인가?
questions.answer1: A region where extreme heat makes growing food impossible.
questions.answer1_ko: 극심한 더위로 식량 재배가 불가능한 지역.
questions.answer2: A farming area that has been abandoned due to soil degradation.
questions.answer2_ko: 토질 저하로 인해 방치된 농경지.
questions.answer3: A low-income neighborhood with limited access to fresh food.
questions.answer3_ko: 신선한 식품에 대한 접근이 제한된 저소득 지역.
questions.answer4: An urban area that lacks the infrastructure for food distribution.
questions.answer4_ko: 식품 유통 인프라가 부족한 도시 지역.
questions.commentary: 3단락에 'low-income neighborhoods that are often classified as food deserts'라고 나옵니다. classified as(~로 분류된)는 described as / referred to as와 같은 의미입니다. 'food desert'가 무엇인지 맥락을 통해 파악하는 것이 핵심입니다.

keywords.meaning: 식량 사막(신선 식품 접근이 어려운 저소득 지역)

questions.quiz: What does paragraph 4 say about urban farming's ability to replace conventional agriculture?
questions.quiz_ko: 4단락에서 도시 농업이 기존 농업을 대체하는 능력에 대해 말하는 것은?
questions.answer1: Urban farming cannot replace conventional agriculture as the primary food source.
questions.answer1_ko: 도시 농업은 주요 식량 공급원으로서 기존 농업을 대체할 수 없다.
questions.answer2: Urban farming will completely replace conventional agriculture within 50 years.
questions.answer2_ko: 도시 농업은 50년 이내에 기존 농업을 완전히 대체할 것이다.
questions.answer3: Urban farming is already producing more food than conventional agriculture in major cities.
questions.answer3_ko: 도시 농업은 이미 주요 도시에서 기존 농업보다 더 많은 식량을 생산하고 있다.
questions.answer4: Experts disagree on whether urban farming can supplement conventional food systems.
questions.answer4_ko: 전문가들은 도시 농업이 기존 식품 체계를 보완할 수 있는지에 대해 의견이 다르다.
questions.commentary: 4단락에 'urban farming alone cannot replace conventional agriculture as the primary source of the world's food supply'라고 명확히 나옵니다. alone(단독으로, 그것만으로)은 제한을 나타내는 부사입니다. supplement(보완하다)는 replace(대체하다)와 반대 의미로 쓰였습니다.

questions.quiz: The word 'scarcity' in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to:

questions.answer1: abundance

questions.answer2: pollution

questions.answer3: demand
questions.answer3_ko: 수요
questions.answer4: shortage
questions.answer4_ko: 부족, 결핍
questions.commentary: scarcity는 '부족, 결핍'이라는 뜻으로, shortage(부족)가 동의어입니다. 'water scarcity'는 '물 부족'을 의미합니다. 반의어는 abundance(풍부함)입니다. scarce(희귀한, 부족한)라는 형용사 형태도 함께 기억해 두세요.

questions.quiz: The word 'intensive' in paragraph 3 is closest in meaning to:

questions.answer2: demanding
